comments.body: DONE — public oracle API shipped (commit d085e8cf). All 4 ACs: (1) three query surfaces over Engine A/T0/Engine B (oracle.refute_absence unifies the lanes incl. dependency→T0; applies(dimension); scan(detectors/dimensions/path_globs)) returning the evidence model+coverage; (2) typed signatures + closed dimension vocab OWNED here (DIMENSIONS_VERSION=1, registry mirrors it CI-guarded), reference schema only exposed (defined in S2); (3) read-only CLI/library/MCP grounding-info tool + grounding_info.schema.json in OUTPUT_SCHEMAS + driven by schema-coverage & MCP-coverage guards; (4) docs/grounding.md + CLAUDE.md Reads list + docs/output-schemas.md refreshed. 198 guard + 18 oracle tests pass; mypy clean. CODE REVIEW (subagent): fixed a BLOCKER (refute_absence raised on a malformed reference — now fails open to abstain(invalid_detector)); facade routing/dimension-consistency/read-tool wiring VERIFIED sound. Closing.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The epic's headline deliverable is a STABLE internal API both review gates consume — one implementation, not
two. This story integrates Engine A, T0, and Engine B behind the three query surfaces and pins the
consumer-facing contract so `5fd2` (DET floor P2/P3/P4) and `9da1` (Pass-2 grounding) can build against it.

## What
- The public **oracle API** exposing the three query surfaces over the engines:
  `refute_absence(reference) -> evidence`, `applies(dimension, repo) -> evidence`, and
  `scan(repo, *, detectors=None, dimensions=None, path_globs=None) -> [evidence]` (job-3 smell/metric scan;
  `None` = all applicable) — each returning the normalized three-valued evidence model + coverage (shape fixed by spike E5).
- The **consumer integration contract**: the reference-in schema (DEFINED + validated in S2 — S5 EXPOSES and
  documents it, never redefines it), and the **closed dimension-ID vocabulary OWNED HERE** (the registry of
  applicability dimensions a consumer passes to `applies()` / a detector declares — initial set
  `{web_frontend, has_iac, touches_auth, has_migrations, has_tests, …}`, versioned), plus the detector identifiers
  and how coverage is surfaced — documented so `5fd2`/`9da1` wire up deterministically.
- **Docs**: how the oracle works; the fail-open behavior + the CLOSED reason enum + coverage semantics; the
  detector envelope format + the `.rebar/detectors/` convention; the **`.rebar/` language-extensibility slot**
  (project ctags optlib / tree-sitter custom grammar) and configurable metric thresholds; the loader's
  pre-validate/quarantine behavior; and the CLI/library/MCP entry points (read-only; mirrors existing rebar
  read-tool patterns).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py` (public surface) + a thin CLI/MCP read-tool exposure + `docs/`.
- Depends on S2 (Engine A), S3 (T0), S4 (Engine B). Pure-evidence: no block/advisory logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stable public API for the three query surfaces over the three engines, returning the evidence model + coverage.
- [ ] Typed signatures for all three surfaces (incl. `scan`'s); the closed dimension-ID vocabulary DEFINED + owned here (consumers/detectors draw from it); reference schema only EXPOSED (defined in S2); coverage surfacing; `5fd2`/`9da1` can build against it.
- [ ] Read-only CLI/library/MCP read-tool entry points consistent with existing rebar read tools — INCLUDING a canonical `.schema.json` in `src/rebar/schemas/`, registration in `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`, and a schema-conformance test (the typed-read-tool convention).
- [ ] Docs updated AND existing docs refreshed (T7): the new grounding read tools/schemas added to the CLAUDE.md MCP "Reads" list + docs/output-schemas.md, alongside API / fail-open + reason enum + coverage / detector format / `.rebar/detectors/` / language-extensibility slot / thresholds / loader-quarantine docs.
compiled_state.title: Public oracle API (3 query surfaces) + consumer integration contract + docs

verify_commands.dd_text: grounding-info read tool emits schema-valid JSON
